page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache architecture includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores fall within the average range, supported by hardware acceleration flags such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. However, multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, register in the weak category. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10% and uncached read/write memory bandwidth classified as weak.

Given its hardware profile, the n4-highcpu-4 is positioned as a budget-conscious option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. It achieves average throughput in static web serving and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, it is poorly suited for memory-intensive database operations or large-scale multi-threaded workloads. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and basic text processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput, but weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth. This server is a cost-efficient option for lightweight, compute-focused workloads such as single-threaded processing, basic text manipulation, and low-traffic web serving, where high memory capacity and intensive multi-core parallelization are not required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and no local storage. It offers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, designed for lightweight compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors with advanced matrix extensions for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration support
Average single-core CPU and static web serving performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
